title: profile page posts not sanitized before HTML interpolation
description: LOCATION: src/server.rs:1315-1326 FINDING: serve_profile_html calls crate::content::process_content(&p.content_html) and interpolates the result directly into the HTML template string (posts_html). process_content produces HTML with tags around mentions and hashtags — it does NOT strip or sanitize the content. The content_html stored in the database was sanitized on ingress (via sanitize_html or markdown_to_html), but process_content may re-introduce href= values derived from the content (e.g., a bare URL linkified to {url}) where url comes from the raw post content. The ammonia sanitization in sanitize::sanitize_html enforces the allowlist. process_content runs AFTER sanitization at ingest time but the profile page does not re-sanitize after process_content. If a future change to process_content or the allowed content generates new tags/attributes, they will appear unsanitized in the HTML profile page. IMPACT: Currently safe because content_html is sanitized on write and process_content only adds tags with class= attributes (which ammonia would strip on re-sanitize). But the defense relies on implicit ordering knowledge, not an explicit guarantee.
